food_subgroup: Pizza — fusion sourdough pizza with Indian-spiced chicken topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific class exists for tandoori chicken pizza or sourdough-base fusion pizza. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific facet for tandoori chicken topping or sourdough base exists in the standard hierarchy. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 is the generic pizza food concept. No specific descendant for tandoori chicken pizza exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual tandoori chicken sourdough pizza, approximately 350 g, as served by Now Pizza at Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat topping (SR Legacy ID 21299) and Pizza, cheese (SR Legacy ID 21298), adapted with sourdough base and Indian-spiced chicken topping components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central for tandoori chicken sourdough pizza. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for meat-topped pizza (FDC ID 21299, ~266 kcal/100g) with adjustments for sourdough base (slightly higher fibre), dual-cheese blend (mozzarella + cheddar, higher saturated fat), and tandoori-spiced chicken tikka topping (higher protein). The resulting per-100g profile reflects a medium-density pizza with moderate protein from chicken and elevated saturated fat from dual cheese. Confidence is medium due to estimation from component analogy.

gi_source.title: Sourdough bread and pizza base GI data — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sourdough bread (GI ~54) used as base analogy for sourdough pizza crust.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tandoori chicken sourdough pizza. GI estimated at 55 based on sourdough bread GI of approximately 54 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Sourdough fermentation lowers GI relative to conventional pizza bases due to organic acid production slowing starch digestion. Protein and fat from cheese and chicken further attenuate postprandial glucose response. GI of 55 assigned (low category, ≤55 threshold). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 99.75) / 100 ≈ 53.5, where carbohydrate per 350g serving = 28.5 × 3.5 = 99.75g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~580 mg/100g; ~2030 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its high sodium load from pizza sauce, dual-cheese blend, and spiced chicken marinade, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium intake exceeding the 2000 mg recommended limit in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 55) and high glycemic load per serving (~53.5) due to large refined-carbohydrate base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the sourdough base confers a lower GI than conventional pizza dough, the large serving size results in a high glycemic load per meal; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion (one to two slices) and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from dual mozzarella-cheddar cheese blend (~4.6 g saturated fat/100g; ~16 g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dual-cheese topping contributes significant saturated fat per serving, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and consider requesting reduced-cheese options to keep saturated fat intake within the recommended 20 g/day ceiling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoodoo Chicki is a fusion sourdough pizza sold by Now Pizza at Funan Mall in Singapore, combining Italian pizza-making tradition with Indian tandoori flavours. The pizza features a sourdough base — which undergoes lactic acid fermentation, resulting in a slightly tangy, chewy crust with a lower glycemic index than conventional pizza dough — topped with Italian tomato pizza sauce, a dual-cheese blend of mozzarella and cheddar, pre-cooked chicken tikka (tandoori-marinated chicken pieces seasoned with yoghurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and chilli), sliced red onions, and jalapeños for heat. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly spicy, and tangy, with the sourdough crust providing a distinctive fermented depth. Nutritionally, the pizza is a moderate-energy food (~255 kcal/100g) with meaningful protein from chicken tikka (~13.5 g/100g), moderate fat from the cheese blend (~9.8 g/100g, of which ~4.6 g is saturated), and carbohydrate primarily from the sourdough base (~28.5 g/100g). Sodium is elevated (~580 mg/100g) due to cheese, sauce, and spice marinade, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension. The GI is estimated at 55 (low category) based on sourdough fermentation data, but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~53.5) due to the large base. Clinically, this dish is moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease, and high concern for hypertension. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. The dish is priced at SGD 16.40 and is available via field survey data from Now Pizza, Funan.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for tandoori chicken sourdough pizza exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy meat-topped pizza data (FDC ID 21299) with component-level adjustments for sourdough base, dual-cheese blend, and chicken tikka topping. GI is estimated by analogy from sourdough bread GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (FDC ID 21299) adapted for sourdough base and tandoori chicken tikka topping; exact laboratory analysis for this specific product is not available in any approved primary source. 2. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sourdough bread GI (~54) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this product exists. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry used for estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry used for estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry used for estimation. 6. Halal status is unconfirmed; Now Pizza's halal certification status was not verifiable from approved sources at time of processing. 7. The food_name 'Tandoori Chicken Pizza' is used as the canonical English name; 'Tandoodoo Chicki' is the restaurant's branded menu name and is preserved as the primary alias. 8. Multilingual names are provided per Singaporean dish requirements (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this dish is sold in Singapore. 9. Serving size of 350g is estimated for an individual pizza; actual weight may vary by restaurant preparation.
